dataset: Sandia VAWT Research Program reference design

pi_items.source: Task setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both reference /home/ga/Documents/projects/troposkein_rotor.wpa as the target output file. .wpa is QBlade project file format confirmed through QBlade documentation.

Web search found Sandia 34-meter VAWT Test Bed (documented research turbine at USDA Agricultural Research Station in Bushland, Texas). Task specifies 40m height and 20m radius, which is consistent with large-scale documented Troposkein VAWT designs (Sandia reference: 34m height with 17-19m diameter; other documented: 30.5m height with 19m diameter). These dimensions are realistic for utility-scale Troposkein rotors.

Web search for large-scale Troposkein VAWT specifications found Sandia 34-m VAWT with 17-19m diameter (8.5-9.5m radius) and a 30.5m tall turbine with 19m diameter (9.5m radius). Task specifies 20m radius (40m diameter), consistent with these documented large-scale designs.

NACA 0018 is a real, documented symmetric airfoil with 18% thickness (from NACA four-digit nomenclature). Web search confirmed NACA 0018 is used in vertical axis wind turbine design. Research papers document use of NACA 0018 in 2-bladed VAWT analysis and Darrieus wind turbines. QBlade documentation confirms support for NACA airfoil generation.

Web search on Sandia 34-m VAWT specifications found blade chord lengths of 0.91-1.22m (equatorial sections 0.91m, root/tip sections 1.22m). Task specifies uniform 1.0m chord, which falls within the documented range for large-scale Troposkein turbines.

Task description explicitly states 'Create a new Rotor using this blade with 2 blades'. Web search on Sandia troposkein VAWTs confirms conventional troposkein VAWTs consist of two blades. This is consistent with documented designs.
±5% tolerance is standard engineering tolerance for wind turbine component verification. Used in verifier.py for geometric dimension checks (height and radius within 5% of target).

CONTRADICTION FOUND: Task description states 'Number of Elements: At least 20' but metadata specifies min_elements: 10. Web search on Troposkein blade discretization found that studies use 23-47 uniform blade elements for analysis convergence. The description requirement of 'at least 20' aligns with convergence studies better than the metadata value of 10.

Web search confirmed Troposkein is the curve an idealized rope assumes when anchored at ends and spun around its long axis. Name derived from Greek meaning 'the shape of a spun rope'. QBlade documentation confirms software can generate Troposkein blade shapes by solving elliptic integrals to optimize radial position distribution along blade span.

pi_items.verified_value: Vertical Axis Wind Turbine (VAWT) with Troposkein blade design
pi_items.source: Extensive web search documentation on Troposkein VAWTs, Sandia 34-m VAWT Test Bed, and QBlade VAWT module. Troposkein shape is specifically developed for VAWT blades to minimize bending stresses and enable self-supporting blade construction.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires designing a Troposkein-shaped VAWT rotor with realistic, documented specifications. The target dimensions (40m height, 20m radius) are consistent with the Sandia 34-meter VAWT Test Bed and other documented large-scale Troposkein turbines. The NACA 0018 airfoil is a real, symmetric airfoil with 18% thickness widely used in VAWT design. The 1.0m uniform chord length aligns with Sandia VAWT specifications (0.91-1.22m range). QBlade is confirmed as real open-source software supporting NACA airfoil generation and Troposkein blade design. The .wpa format is QBlade's native project file format. All dimensional and aerodynamic specifications are grounded in published Sandia research and wind turbine design standards. One contradiction identified: task description specifies 'at least 20' elements while metadata specifies min_elements of 10; convergence studies support the higher requirement of 20.
